commands.review_summary:
The PR adds DeepSeek balance fetching and summary-only provider usage snapshots, wires DeepSeek API-key usage auth into status/auth-status surfaces, updates SDK docs/baselines, and adds focused tests plus small type-check cleanups.

PR surface: Source +160, Tests +346, Docs +10, Generated 0. Total +516 across 30 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a broken existing behavior report. Source inspection shows current main lacks DeepSeek in provider usage registration and lacks summary-only snapshots, while the PR body supplies live endpoint output for the new path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer/API-contract acceptance, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat(deepseek): show provider balance in usage status
target.body:
## What

- Registers DeepSeek as a provider usage source and wires the DeepSeek provider plugin to resolve API-key usage auth.
- Adds a DeepSeek balance fetcher for `GET /user/balance`, exposed through the plugin SDK provider-usage subpath.
- Adds `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` so balance-only providers can report provider text without pretending it is a quota window.
- Shows summary-only usage in `/status`, status reports, and the models auth status usage enrichment for DeepSeek API-key profiles.

## Why

After the provider plugin architecture moved provider-specific usage logic into plugins, DeepSeek was not wired into usage snapshots. Users could see local session costs, but `/status` and related status surfaces could not show the DeepSeek account balance.

DeepSeek balance is not a reset-window quota, so this keeps it as provider-reported summary text instead of fabricating a percent-left window.

## Impact

DeepSeek API-key users can now see balance text such as `Balance ¥42.50` in usage/status surfaces. Existing quota-window providers continue to use `windows`; summary-only formatting is only used when no windows are present.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: DeepSeek API-key usage snapshots now return provider balance text and the summary-only formatter displays it instead of dropping providers with no quota windows.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout of this PR branch at `07beef3339a3035a13de3010c48cbaf2de26ecbc`, Node/tsx runtime, real DeepSeek API key from local secret storage. Secret and balance amount were redacted from the captured output.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local Node/tsx snippet that imports `fetchDeepSeekUsage` from `src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.ts`, wraps `fetch` to capture only safe request metadata, calls the live DeepSeek `https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance` endpoint with the real API key, then passes the returned snapshot through `formatUsageWindowSummary`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
DeepSeek usage live proof
head=07beef3339a3035a13de3010c48cbaf2de26ecbc
endpoint=https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance
method=GET
authorizationHeaderPresent=true
accept=application/json
provider=deepseek
displayName=DeepSeek
windows=0
hasSummary=true
summary=Balance ¥<redacted>
formatted=Balance ¥<redacted>
error=none
```

- Observed result after fix: The live DeepSeek response produced a `ProviderUsageSnapshot` with `provider=deepseek`, zero quota windows, a non-empty balance summary, and no error. The formatter returned the same redacted balance text instead of `null`.
- What was not tested: Full gateway UI/dashboard manual click path and invalid-key live error handling were not exercised in this live proof. The exact balance amount is intentionally redacted.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This uses a real DeepSeek account balance endpoint, but the local account balance value cannot be shared publicly. Automated unit tests cover the mocked error and formatting edge cases separately.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, DeepSeek was absent from `usageProviders` and had no provider usage hook, so `/status` and usage surfaces had no DeepSeek balance snapshot to format.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/tts/tts-core.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts extensions/deepseek/index.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-internal && pnpm lint:extensions:no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...touched TS files...`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/usage-tracking.md`
- `git diff --check`
